items.docs:
Creates a new order and verification. Returns a JSON object representing the newly created order and verification.

A target employer verification is distinct in that it _requires_ a target employer in the request body for processing.

## Verification processing

Target employer verifications are processed _asynchronously_: they will be created and returned in the response body without any [reports](api.yaml/paths/~1verification-requests~1{verification_id}~1report/get). Truework will then send a

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated names of fields to include in the response. Separate multiple fields with commas, and denote subfields with parentheses. Multiple subfields from the same type can be listed within parentheses. In case of a list, subfields apply to each individual object. If omitted, all fields are included.

params.name: include_income_analytics
params.description: Whether to calculate income analytics for the nested reports in each verification.

params.name: include_report_annotations
params.description: Include potentially unverified data in the response, including report annotations and unparsed documents.

items.docs:
POST requests create both an order and a verification. Returns a JSON object representing the newly created order and verification.

## Verification processing

Employer search verifications are processed _synchronously_: Truework will attempt to process the verification during the initial POST request. If successful, the `201` response will include a `reports` key, which will contain the requested data.

It is recommended to set a timeout on synchronous requests, to account for potential latenc
POST requests create an order with a Truework Direct session token, used for instantiating the Truework.js widget.

params.name: include_report_annotations
params.description: Include potentially unverified data in the response, including report annotations and unparsed documents.

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of results returned

params.name: search_key
params.description: Filter by search_key value passed in on Order creation

params.name: include_report_annotations
params.description: Opt-in to including annotations on reports

items.docs: An order is a consolidation of multiple verifications.
Cancels all verification requests on an order, provided each request is not already in a terminal state.

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of results returned

items.docs: Returns all events associated with an order.
Reverifies a report, provided no more than 90 days have passed since completion of the verification.

params.name: include_income_analytics
params.description: Whether to calculate income analytics for the nested reports in each verification.

params.name: include_report_annotations
params.description: Whether to include annotations for the nested reports in each verification.

params.name: fields
params.description: Comma-separated names of fields to include in the response. Separate multiple fields with commas, and denote subfields with parentheses. Multiple subfields from the same type can be listed within parentheses. In case of a list, subfields apply to each individual object. If omitted, all fields are included.

items.docs:
Retrieves a report for a given report ID.
If you don't have the report id, it is recommended to use the embedded reports key on a completed verification object returned from `GET /orders`.
You can get the report in PDF format by adding `Accept: application/pdf` to your headers. When testing, please use the generated curl command
with `--output <PDF filename>` specified.

<Info>
Only completed Reports will be returned. Any other request will fail with either 400 or 404, and provide more informati
